question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted numeric topic ID to findById() that resolves to a deleted or historically superseded Topic record, causing the mirror node to serve stale/tampered Hashgraph history as current authoritative state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Long-typed primary key in findById() by supplying a negative or zero topic ID through the /api/v1/topics/{id} endpoint, causing the repository to return an unexpected Topic entity whose timestampRange reflects manipulated Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user craft a topic ID that, after EntityId.getId() encoding (shard/realm/num bit-packing), collides with a different topic's internal Long ID, causing findById() to return a Topic with a falsified timestampRange that misrepresents Hashgraph consensus history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of timestamp-range validation in findById() to retrieve a Topic whose timestampRange has an unbounded upper end (Range.atLeast), causing consumers to incorrectly treat a historically closed topic as currently active, thereby misrepresenting Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user trigger findById() with a topic ID that exists in the topic_history table but not in the topic table, and if the repository silently returns empty rather than the historical record, cause downstream systems to incorrectly conclude the topic never existed, effectively erasing Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findById() only queries the topic table (not topic_history) to obtain a Topic whose adminKey field reflects a post-update state, causing the API to misrepresent the historical adminKey that was in effect at a specific consensus timestamp, thereby tampering with the perceived Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findById() returns a Topic with a submitKey that reflects the latest state rather than the historical state at a given consensus timestamp, causing fee calculators or validators to apply incorrect key constraints that misrepresent Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of a deleted-entity filter in findById() to retrieve a Topic entity for a topic that was deleted on-chain, causing the mirror node to serve active-looking topic data that contradicts the true Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the Long overflow boundary in findById() by supplying a topic ID of Long.MAX_VALUE through the EntityIdNumParameter path, causing the repository to execute a query with a wrapped/negative ID that returns an unintended Topic record with falsified history fields?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findById() returns a Topic whose createdTimestamp field (marked @Column(updatable = false)) could be inconsistent with the timestampRange lower bound, causing downstream consumers to reconstruct a false Hashgraph history timeline for the topic?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the FeeTopicStore.getTopic() path, which calls findById(id.topicNum()) using only the topic number without shard/realm validation, to retrieve a Topic from a different shard/realm that has a different historical adminKey, thereby causing fee calculations to be based on tampered Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the FeeTopicStore.getTopic() method's use of findById(id.topicNum()) — which ignores shard and realm — to cause a cross-shard topic ID collision that returns a Topic with a feeScheduleKey from a different network's Hashgraph history, corrupting fee enforcement?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findById() returns a Topic with a feeExemptKeyList that reflects the current state rather than the historical state at a specific consensus timestamp, causing the fee exemption logic to be applied based on manipulated Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of consensus-timestamp-scoped queries in findById() to retrieve a Topic whose feeScheduleKey was updated after a specific block, causing the mirror node to serve a fee schedule that misrepresents the Hashgraph history at that block height?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the CrudRepository.findById() method's lack of row-level locking to observe a Topic in a partially-updated state (torn read) during an upsert operation, causing the returned timestampRange or adminKey to reflect an inconsistent intermediate Hashgraph state?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the @Upsertable(history = true) annotation's upsert behavior — where a Topic update moves the old record to topic_history — to cause a race condition where findById() returns the pre-history record during the upsert window, serving stale Hashgraph history as current?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the EntityIdNumParameter regex (^((\\d{1,4})\\.)?((\\d{1,5})\\.)?(\\d{1,12})$) to supply a topic ID with leading zeros (e.g., 0.0.000001) that parses to a different Long value than expected, causing findById() to return a Topic with a different historical timestampRange than the user intended to query?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the TopicMapper's mapping of entity.timestampRange to the API response timestamp field — where the Topic's own timestampRange is ignored — to cause the API to serve a timestamp that does not match the Topic's actual Hashgraph history record, creating a false historical narrative?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that TopicController calls topicService.findById(), entityService.findById(), and customFeeService.findById() independently without a shared transaction, causing the three data sources to reflect different consensus timestamps and thus serve a composite response that misrepresents Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the non-transactional nature of the three independent findById() calls in TopicController (topic, entity, customFee) to observe a topic in a state where the Topic record has been updated to a new timestampRange but the Entity record still reflects the old one, producing a historically inconsistent response that misrepresents Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of shard/realm validation in TopicServiceImpl.findById() — which calls topicRepository.findById(id.getId()) using the encoded Long — to retrieve a Topic from a different shard that has a different historical adminKey, causing the mirror node to serve cross-shard Hashgraph history as if it were from the expected shard?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findById() returns a Topic with a null createdTimestamp (since it is nullable per @Column(updatable = false)) to cause downstream history reconstruction logic to treat the topic as having no creation event in Hashgraph history, effectively erasing its origin?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findById() returns a Topic with a null adminKey (byte[] adminKey is nullable in AbstractTopic) to cause fee enforcement or key-validation logic to treat the topic as having no admin key in its Hashgraph history, bypassing historical key constraints?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findById() returns a Topic with a null submitKey to cause the FeeTopicStore to construct a Topic protobuf with no submit key, causing the fee calculator to apply incorrect fee logic that contradicts the Hashgraph history of the topic's submit key?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/TopicRepository.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: findById()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that findById() returns a Topic with a null feeScheduleKey to cause the FeeTopicStore to construct a Topic protobuf that omits the fee schedule key, causing fee calculations to be based on a falsified view of the topic's Hashgraph history?
